history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L4_to_L5.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Sword-specific weapon beats applied at L3, L4, L5, L6, L7, L10 (see WEAPON_BEAT_OVERRIDES in build_sword_prompts.py for the per-level deltas).
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2): two inputs = L4 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L4 'Conscript Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every L2 + L3 + L4 feature shown in the first input image: (a) QUILTED BROWN LEATHER SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms — the L2 quilted leather under-tunic stays UNDER the L4 scale-mail sleeves; (b) WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle; (c) BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap and brass stud on anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT); (d) BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH with cream bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip (viewer-RIGHT); (e) TWO STEEL PAULDRON CAPS — one on each shoulder, with three or four dark-iron rivets along each pauldron's lower seam; (f) BRASS QUILLON CAPS at the cross-guard tips (small warm-brass bulb caps at each end of the cross-guard, distinctly warm brass tone against the cool steel cross-guard) — these MUST REMAIN at this level; (g) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS radiating from the central steel boss in a cross pattern on the wooden shield face; (h) SCALE-MAIL SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms (chunky overlapping rounded scales matching the mail coif); (i) L4 LONGER CROSS-GUARD with downward-curled quillon tips plus DEEPENED CENTRAL FULLER on the sword blade (between and around the blade — at L5 these rings stay PRESENT on the sword blade, do not remove them); (j) CONTINUOUS DARK-IRON RIM BAND wrapping the entire shield perimeter; (k) STEEL CHEST-STRAP RIVET PLATE centered on the upper chest with four dark-iron rivets. DO NOT revert any of these features. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, marking the mid Tier 2 step from Conscript Sergeant to Sergeant. The unit moves from leather/scale-sleeve kit to a partially-plated upper body while keeping the red+mustard split livery visible AS A CHEST BAND between the new partial breastplate (above) and the existing leather belt (below). (1) STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD — replace the WOODEN PLANK FACE of the round shield with a smooth painted-steel skin in cool silver-gray with faint vertical brushed-steel highlights running across the face. The wooden plank grain is FULLY REPLACED — no wood visible across the shield face. The existing four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss remain visible as raised dark-iron features on top of the new steel face; the existing continuous iron rim band remains; the existing eight L2 dark-iron rim rivets remain just inside the rim; the central cool-gray steel boss is now slightly larger and more domed than at L4 (~3.5 inches up from 3 inches). The shield silhouette stays the same round shape; only the face material changes from wood to steel. (2) STEEL PARTIAL BREASTPLATE COVERING ONLY THE UPPER CHEST — add a smooth cool silver-gray steel breastplate piece covering ONLY the upper chest from collarbone down to roughly the bottom of the sternum (covering approximately the top half of the chest area). The breastplate has a slight medial ridge running vertically down the center. The lower edge of the breastplate is a clean horizontal line slightly above the level of the existing chest-strap rivet plate. CRITICAL: the LIVERY TUNIC remains FULLY VISIBLE BELOW the breastplate — the red+mustard split tunic now reads as a CHEST BAND between the breastplate's lower edge (above) and the wide leather belt (below). The livery band is approximately 3 inches tall and visibly shows BOTH the viewer-LEFT solid crimson-red half AND the viewer-RIGHT mustard-yellow half with the vertical crimson-red center stripe. Below the belt the livery tunic skirt also remains fully visible reaching mid-thigh. The L4 chest-strap rivet plate is now incorporated into / hidden by the new breastplate at the upper chest. (3) STEEL VAMBRACES REPLACING THE LEATHER FOREARM BRACERS — replace the two existing brown leather forearm bracers with cool silver-gray polished steel vambraces wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms. Each vambrace is a single piece of curved steel plate with two visible dark-iron rivets at each seam edge. The leather bracers are FULLY GONE — no warm-brown leather visible on either forearm. The hands remain bare warm peachy-tan skin (anatomical-RIGHT hand grips the sword grip; anatomical-LEFT hand and forearm hidden behind the shield). (4) HEAVIER WHEEL POMMEL PLUS ETCHED MAKER'S MARK ON THE RICASSO — REPLACE the L1 plain disc pommel at the bottom of the leather grip with a heavier WHEEL POMMEL: a thicker disc-shaped steel pommel approximately 2 inches diameter (up from 1.5 inches), with a slightly raised central boss, cool silver-gray polished steel. ALSO add a small ETCHED MAKER'S MARK on the ricasso (the unsharpened section at the base of the blade just above the cross-guard) — a stylized geometric mark approximately ½ inch tall, etched into the steel as a tonal incised detail (no color, just a darker incised line on the steel surface).  (5) STEEL BEVOR / HALF-COLLAR — add a short cool silver-gray steel half-collar plate covering the front of the throat from the collarbone up to the lower jaw line, sitting over the front of the mail coif's collar drape. The bevor curves with the neck (concave shape facing the viewer) and adds visible volume at the upper chest silhouette. Top edge of the bevor reaches the lower lip of the beard; bottom edge sits just above the new breastplate's collarbone line. The bevor connects visually with the breastplate but is a separate piece (a thin shadow line is visible at the seam). The mail coif's neck-back drape remains visible behind the bevor. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with single bright catchlight; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin with rosy blush; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (now framed at the bottom by the new bevor; beard remains visible above the bevor's top edge) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache; his dark-brown short hair as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering scalp, ears, and back-of-neck drape (front-of-throat now covered by the new bevor; the rest of the coif drape remains); the LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT solid crimson-red half, viewer-RIGHT mustard-yellow half with vertical crimson-red center stripe — now reading as a CHEST BAND between breastplate and belt PLUS continuing as the visible skirt below the belt; the wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the two steel pauldron caps; the leather grip wrap on the upper sword grip; the L4 longer cross-guard with downward-curled quillon tips and deepened blade fuller; the arming sword held in the anatomical-RIGHT hand — sword leather grip, sword blade with iron banding (L4) plus new central reinforcement plate (L5), simple steel cross-guard, sword blade extending downward and toward viewer-LEFT with the blade tip near hip height; the round shield held in anatomical-LEFT arm — now steel-faced (per Edit Goal item 1) but same round shape, same iron cross strap-bands, same iron rim band, same rim rivets, same boss; the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers; the warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent ~120 degrees, hand grips the sword's leather grip, sword blade dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, forearm angled across the body, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height with face presenting toward viewer at slight three-quarter angle. Calm guard stance — both arms static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the SECOND input image exactly — L1_Base scale anchor) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall), approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide). TOP of mail coif at ~16.6% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at ~16.6% from bottom of canvas. Character vertically centered. ~18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, ~20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT remove the LIVERY tunic — the red+mustard split MUST remain visibly present as a CHEST BAND between the partial breastplate and the leather belt AND continuing as the visible skirt below the belt; DO NOT cover the entire chest with the breastplate — the breastplate covers ONLY the upper chest from collarbone to roughly the bottom of the sternum at L5; DO NOT extend the breastplate down to the belt or to the abdomen — full breastplate extension arrives at L6; DO NOT shrink the livery band into a thin strip narrower than ~3 inches; DO NOT replace the livery with a body coat, gambeson, surcoat, or any cloth other than the existing red+mustard split; DO NOT add tassets, fauld plates, hip plates, or any waist / hip armor — those arrive at L6; DO NOT add steel sabatons, greaves, leg armor — sabatons arrive at L6; DO NOT add a helm, sallet, kettle helm, or any structural addition to the head — head covering remains the existing mail coif (L7 introduces the open-face sallet); DO NOT add a closed visor, half-visor, or any face cover — face remains fully visible; DO NOT add scale-mail body coat or extend the existing scale-mail sleeves down past the bicep — sleeves stay short at L5 (full sleeve / forearm coverage by scale arrives at L6 as the body fills); DO NOT extend the scale-mail upward to cover the chest under the breastplate — the chest under the breastplate is the existing livery; DO NOT add a second sword, second shield, second weapon; DO NOT replace the sword blade with a flanged steel head — the wooden head + spikes + L4 iron banding + new L5 wheel pommel is the L5 sword; flanged steel arrives at L7; DO NOT change the cross-guard shape on the sword blade; DO NOT add iron banding to the sword grip — that arrives at L6; DO NOT replace or remove the leather grip wrap on the sword; DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, square shield, or any non-round shape; DO NOT add embossed sun-burst boss or detailed boss pattern — that arrives at L7; DO NOT change the boss color to gold or add any gold to the shield; DO NOT add gauntlets — gauntlets arrive at L7; DO NOT make the steel vambraces extend into gauntlets — vambraces stop at the wrist, hands remain bare; DO NOT make the pauldrons layered multi-disc — at L5 each pauldron is a single rounded steel cap (slightly larger than L4); layered 2-disc pauldrons arrive at L6; DO NOT add gold trim, gold rivets, gold band, gold buckle, gold accents, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at L8; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT add horns, crests, plumes, or vertical helm extensions; DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the hair, the coif color, the trouser color, or the boot color; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
